pitch: Cuts AI inference spend by routing each request to the cheapest model and GPU mix that still meets SLAs.
problem: Teams lose margin because model choice, batching, and infra are tuned manually instead of continuously.

targetUser.primary: AI product teams running production inference
targetUser.buyer: CTO or head of AI platform

solution: A policy engine that routes requests across models, regions, and GPU pools, then auto-tunes for cost, latency, and quality.

whyNow.point: Frontier model funding is pushing inference scale and compute efficiency into the budget line.
signalRefs: Frontier AI money is getting bigger and more strategic

whyNow.point: More AI workloads are moving from pilots to production, where small latency and cost gains matter.
signalRefs: Frontier AI money is getting bigger and more strategic

differentiator: Workload-aware routing plus closed-loop optimization instead of static cost dashboards.

conceptDiagram.title: Inference cost control loop
conceptDiagram.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Traffic --> Router --> CheapestFit
  CheapestFit --> SLOs
  SLOs --> Router

signalStrength.rationale: Directly tied to the biggest compute and funding signal.

painIntensity.rationale: Inference spend is immediate, visible, and recurring.

wedgeClarity.rationale: Clear ROI wedge through cost and latency optimization.

defensibility.rationale: Workflow data and routing policies improve with usage.

ventureScale.rationale: Large TAM across every production AI team.

topRisks.name: Platform incumbents
topRisks.description: Cloud vendors may bundle similar routing features.
topRisks.mitigation: Focus on multi-cloud and multi-model optimization.

topRisks.name: Benchmark trust
topRisks.description: Customers may doubt routing decisions without proof.
topRisks.mitigation: Ship transparent policy controls and audit logs.
